other_observers: Tom and Mary France; I saw and identified the bird first. My companions, Tom and Mary France, then saw the bird and agreed with the identification. Tom was able to get a photo of it in the marsh on the N side of the road.
specific_location: First seen in the wetland on the S side of WCR 48 between WCR 43 and 47. It then flew NE over us and landed in the marsh on the N side of WCR 48.
description: Heron shape, similar in size to a Little Blue Heron. White belly, blue-gray wings, back and neck.Behaviors: Hunting in a marsh, heron style.Call: Not callingPlumage: adult
photographer: Photographer: Tom France, Tom France, Tom France
